notes:
BW6 Dragons Exalted, 2012 Black & White era. Pack = 10 cards: 5 common, 3 uncommon, 1 reverse-holo, 1 rare slot. Box = 36 packs (no ETB existed for this set; standalone ETB products were not produced for BW6, so etbPacks=null). 

Rare-slot distribution mapped onto this set's buckets {rare, rare-holo, ex, ultra-rare, secret-rare}. Spine taken from thepricedex per-box counts (rare-holo 8.7/box, ex 2/box, secret 0.3/box, i.e. ~1 secret per 3-4 boxes) cross-checked against pokeden (Full Art 1-in-18, i.e. ~2/box) and community box reports on PokeBeach/pokegym (typical box = 2-4 EX total, 0-3 of them Full Art, occasional secret). Per-pack rare slot: rare 0.6521, rare-holo 0.242, ex (non-FA Pokemon-EX) 0.0556 (~2/box), ultra-rare (Full Art EX) 0.042 (~1.5/box), secret-rare 0.0083 (~0.3/box). Sums to 1.0.

REJECTED pokeden's secret-rare figure (5% / 1-in-20) — it contradicts thepricedex (1-in-108), the community consensus of roughly 1 secret per 3-4 boxes, and pokegym/PokeBeach box reports; pokeden appears to misclassify. Also corrected PackRip's pack structure ('4 common + 1 energy'): BW-era English packs are 5 common + 1 reverse holo with no fixed energy slot.

Reverse-holo slot distribution (common 0.5 / uncommon 0.42 / rare 0.08) is an era-typical approximation proportional to bucket sizes for the non-holo reverse pool; reverse-holo did not commonly upgrade to ex/ultra/secret in BW6, so those are excluded from that slot.

Confidence = medium: backed by multiple aggregator pull-rate pages and consistent community box reports, but no single large published per-set study (TPCi had randomized, non-guaranteed rates in this era). Vintage sealed BW6 box carries a large collector premium, so per-pack EV will sit well below current sealed box price — expected, not a rate error.
